title: gardenia ternifolia subsp. ternifolia
intro: Gardenia ternifolia subsp. ternifolia, also known as the Cape gardenia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Gardenia ternifolia subsp. ternifolia include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve digestion, and act as an antimicrobial agent, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve infusions, decoctions, or infusions of the leaves, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or salves for medicinal purposes.
